free_text: Scattering of 41 MeV alpha particles and 46 MeV
He-3 from Al-27, Si-28, Si-29 and Si-30.
The silicon isotope targets were in the form of silicon
oxide evaporated onto plastic backing.
Enrichments of the principal isotopes were 99.8%, 95.3%
and 95.6%, respectively for the Si-28, Si-29 and Si-30
targets. Target thicknesses of silicon as determined by
measuring elastic scattering of 9 MeV protons were
139-, 162- and 120-mug/cm**2, respectively, for Si-28,
Si-29 and Si-30.
The oxygen and carbon impurities limited measurements
of elastic scattering to angles beyond approx.
24-degree and inelastic scattering to angles beyond
approx. 45-degree.
The aluminum target was a self-supporting foil
950-mug/cm**2 as determined by alpha-particle energy
loss in the foil.
Four silicon surface barrier detectors were
used for the alpha-particle measurements.
Four telescopes of delta-E and E detectors
of 300- and 2500-mum thickness, respectively for the
He-3 measurements.
Beam intensities was 150 nA on target
-By authors.The alpha-particle data back of 150-degree
are from C.B.Fulmer(PR/C,13,937,1976)
